name: San Luis Potosí (Mexico)

colors.symbolism: the field and details of the arms
the mineral wealth of the state and details of the arms
the Cerro de San Pedro and details of the arms

description: A white field with the coat of arms of the state of San Luis Potosí centred.
history: San Luis Potosí has no officially adopted state flag; the design shown — a white field bearing the state coat of arms — is in de facto use to represent the state.

features.symbolism: A white field charged with the state coat of arms, in the typical pattern of Mexican state flags.

features.role: coat of arms of San Luis Potosí
features.symbolism: The arms commemorate Saint Louis IX of France (patron of the city) atop the Cerro de San Pedro, whose silver mines gave the city the second part of its name (after the Andean Potosí), together with references to the foundation of the city in 1592.
